vision_report.compositional_truth_check: Registry vs. Reality: P1 (Yellow Pot) is present but appears devoid of foliage. P2/P4 (Black Pot) contains a single, struggling specimen. P3 (Black Pot) contains the rabbit anchor and a single leaf. Registry is largely inaccurate regarding current biomass.

plant_audit.P1: Empty yellow pot. No signs of String of Nickels.
plant_audit.P2_P4: Empty black pot. No signs of Mexican Mint or Silver Guest.
plant_audit.P3: Single leaf remaining. Shows signs of chlorosis and potential dehydration.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots; no visible surface cracking.
biome_observations.debris: Significant presence of crushed eggshells used as a soil amendment/top dressing.
biome_observations.anomalies: The desk surface shows no new growth; the environment appears stagnant.

temporal_deltas.t-5_to_t-4: P1 appears to have lost its label or it has been obscured; P3 leaf remains static.
temporal_deltas.t-4_to_t-3: Introduction of a white cable/wire near the yellow pot.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: No change. The system is in a state of arrested development.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The specimens are in a state of severe decline. P3 is the only pot with remaining biomass, but the leaf is pale and lacks turgor. The lack of growth over 5 days suggests root failure or extreme environmental stress.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of crushed eggshells is the primary intervention noted. The absence of the registered plants suggests a failure of the biome.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical collection is in a critical state of decline. The registry does not match the visual evidence, as most pots are effectively empty or contain only remnants of previous specimens. The environment is static, with no signs of new growth or recovery. The white rabbit anchor remains the only constant in an otherwise failing ecosystem.
